title: A Fast Crocodile Relative from Long Ago
summary: Scientists found a new type of crocodile relative that lived 215 million years ago in Gloucester, UK. Unlike today's crocodiles, this animal lived on land and could run fast like a greyhound. It had long, skinny legs and a light body to move quickly through plants, hunting small animals like reptiles and amphibians. The area was hot and dry with high ground. The species is named after a knight from stories and a teacher who inspired the lead scientist. Fossils were found in old cave deposits in Wales and England, where dead animals washed underground and got buried. This discovery helps us learn about life before dinosaurs.

questions.question: What time period did this animal live in?

background_read: This article is about a discovery from long ago. Scientists study old bones called fossils to learn about animals that lived millions of years ago. Crocodiles today live in water, but this relative lived on land and could run fast. The area was hot and dry, and the animal hunted small creatures. Naming it after a knight and a teacher shows how scientists honor people who inspire them. Finding fossils in caves helps us understand ancient ecosystems.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and a student named Ewan Bodenham
WHAT: Found a new fast land-dwelling crocodile relative from 215 million years ago
WHERE: Gloucester, UK, and cave deposits in Wales and England
WHY: To learn about ancient animals and ecosystems before dinosaurs

perspectives.description: Finding new species helps us understand evolution and ancient life.
